description:
`docs/nginx-sample.conf` was added when nginx was decoupled to /opt/edge/ (2026-05-14). Two patterns flagged by audit #2:

1. **Lines 35, 71** — `proxy_set_header Host $host` accepts upstream-controlled Host. The operative config at /opt/edge/ uses Cloudflare-controlled Host; the sample should reflect that.
2. **Line 85** — `proxy_http_version 1.1` + line 87 `proxy_set_header Connection $connection_upgrade` BUT does not include the corresponding `map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade { default upgrade; '' close; }` block. A fork copying only this file gets `Connection: upgrade` always-on, which re-introduces h2c smuggling (same class as VVP-2026-014 fixed earlier).

Risk to deployed instance: zero (operative config is /opt/edge/ and correct).
Risk to forks following the README: medium.

Fix:
- Add the `map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade {...}` block to the sample.
- Document the Host-header contract in a comment.
- Optionally: add a short README note explaining that this sample is single-app/single-vhost only and the production vigilant deployment uses a separate shared edge stack.

After fix, mark-attempted then re-run /audit to flip the finding.
title: Harden docs/nginx-sample.conf (VVP-2026-020)
